author: Dmytro Mishkin
message:
Windows cibuildwheel: install OpenBLAS/LAPACK via vcpkg and fix MSVC compile by replacing GNU min/max macros

### Motivation
- Windows wheel builds failed to locate LAPACK when only OpenBLAS was installed via `vcpkg`, causing CMake configuration failures on Windows runners.
- The vcpkg toolchain path needed to be made explicit so CMake can discover the vcpkg buildsystem on Windows CI.
- Sources used GCC statement-expression `min`/`max` macros that are incompatible with MSVC and caused syntax/undeclared-identifier errors during Windows builds.
- Ensure these Windows-targeted changes do not break Linux or macOS workflows.

### Description
- Add a `build_wheels_windows` job to `.github/workflows/build_wheels.yml` that runs on `windows-latest` and uses `cibuildwheel` to build/upload Windows wheels.
- Install BLAS and LAPACK on Windows CI by setting `CIBW_BEFORE_ALL_WINDOWS` to `vcpkg install openblas lapack --triplet x64-windows` and expose the vcpkg toolchain using `CIBW_ENVIRONMENT_WINDOWS: "VCPKG_ROOT=C:/vcpkg CMAKE_TOOLCHAIN_FILE=C:/vcpkg/scripts/buildsystems/vcpkg.cmake"`.
- Replace GNU statement-expression `min`/`max` macros in `src/pydegensac/degensac/exp_ranF.c` and `src/pydegensac/degensac/exp_ranH.c` with portable guarded definitions (`#ifndef min ... #endif` / `#ifndef max ... #endif`) to be compatible with MSVC.

### Testing
- No CI workflow runs were executed for this change (no GitHub Actions runs produced yet).
- No automated unit tests were run as part of this PR.

author: Dmytro Mishkin
message:
Windows cibuildwheel: install OpenBLAS+LAPACK and set explicit vcpkg toolchain path

### Motivation
- Windows wheel builds failed because CMake could not find LAPACK when only OpenBLAS was installed via `vcpkg`, causing wheel build errors.
- Ensure `vcpkg` toolchain is discoverable on Windows runners to avoid empty/incorrect environment expansion during CMake configuration.
- Provide BLAS/LAPACK libraries on Windows CI so wheels build successfully with native dependencies.

### Description
- Add a new `build_wheels_windows` job to `.github/workflows/build_wheels.yml` to run `cibuildwheel` on Windows runners using `windows-latest`.
- Install both OpenBLAS and LAPACK via `vcpkg` by setting `CIBW_BEFORE_ALL_WINDOWS` to `vcpkg install openblas lapack --triplet x64-windows`.
- Set `CIBW_ENVIRONMENT_WINDOWS` to an explicit toolchain path: `"VCPKG_ROOT=C:/vcpkg CMAKE_TOOLCHAIN_FILE=C:/vcpkg/scripts/buildsystems/vcpkg.cmake"` so CMake can locate the vcpkg buildsystem.
- Preserve artifact upload and GitHub release steps for the Windows wheel job (`wheels-windows`).

### Testing
- No CI workflow runs were executed after this workflow change, so no integration CI results are available.
- No automated unit tests were run as part of this change.
- Local or remote build logs shown in the rollout motivated the addition of `lapack` because CMake failed to find `LAPACK_LIBRARIES` before this change.
- The workflow file syntax was updated and committed, but no verification runs were performed on GitHub Actions.

author: Dmytro Mishkin
message:
Fix Windows cibuildwheel vcpkg toolchain path and install OpenBLAS

### Motivation
- Windows `cibuildwheel` builds failed because CMake could not find the `vcpkg` toolchain file due to incorrect/empty path expansion on Windows runners.
- Provide OpenBLAS on Windows CI so BLAS/LAPACK dependencies are available to CMake during wheel builds.
- Avoid backslash/escaping and environment expansion issues by using an explicit Windows-style vcpkg root path (`C:/vcpkg`).
- Preserve existing artifact upload and GitHub release behavior for built wheels.

### Description
- Add a `build_wheels_windows` job in `.github/workflows/build_wheels.yml` to run `cibuildwheel` on Windows runners.
- Install OpenBLAS before building by setting `CIBW_BEFORE_ALL_WINDOWS` to `vcpkg install openblas --triplet x64-windows`.
- Set `CIBW_ENVIRONMENT_WINDOWS` to an explicit path: `"VCPKG_ROOT=C:/vcpkg CMAKE_TOOLCHAIN_FILE=C:/vcpkg/scripts/buildsystems/vcpkg.cmake"` to ensure CMake can locate `vcpkg.cmake`.
- Keep artifact upload and GitHub release steps for the Windows wheel job.

### Testing
- No CI workflow runs were executed after this workflow change.
- No automated unit tests were run as part of this change.

author: Dmytro Mishkin
message:
Fix Windows cibuildwheel vcpkg toolchain path and add OpenBLAS install

### Motivation
- Windows `cibuildwheel` builds failed because CMake could not find the vcpkg toolchain file due to backslash escaping in the environment string.
- Provide OpenBLAS on Windows CI so BLAS/LAPACK dependencies are available to CMake during `cibuildwheel` builds.
- Ensure `CMAKE_TOOLCHAIN_FILE` environment entry is passed in a way that preserves the vcpkg path on Windows runners.

### Description
- Add a `build_wheels_windows` job in `.github/workflows/build_wheels.yml` to run `cibuildwheel` on Windows runners.
- Install OpenBLAS prior to building by setting `CIBW_BEFORE_ALL_WINDOWS` to `vcpkg install openblas --triplet x64-windows`.
- Fix `CIBW_ENVIRONMENT_WINDOWS` to a double-quoted string and use forward slashes for the toolchain file: `CMAKE_TOOLCHAIN_FILE=${{ env.VCPKG_INSTALLATION_ROOT }}/scripts/buildsystems/vcpkg.cmake` to avoid backslash escaping issues.
- Keep artifact upload and GitHub release steps for the Windows wheel job.

### Testing
- A prior `cibuildwheel` run failed with CMake error: `Could not find toolchain file: scriptsbuildsystemsvcpkg.cmake` (demonstrating the original escaping problem).
- No CI workflow runs were executed after this workflow change.
- No unit tests were run as part of this change.

author: Dmytro Mishkin
message:
Fix Windows cibuildwheel vcpkg toolchain path and install OpenBLAS via vcpkg

### Motivation
- Windows wheel builds failed because CMake could not find the vcpkg toolchain file due to incorrect escaping of backslashes in the environment string.
- Provide OpenBLAS on Windows CI so BLAS/LAPACK dependencies are available to CMake during `cibuildwheel` builds.
- Ensure `CMAKE_TOOLCHAIN_FILE` environment entry points to `scripts\buildsystems\vcpkg.cmake` on Windows runners.

### Description
- Update `.github/workflows/build_wheels.yml` to add a Windows `build_wheels_windows` job that runs `cibuildwheel` on Windows runners.
- Set `CIBW_BEFORE_ALL_WINDOWS` to `vcpkg install openblas --triplet x64-windows` so OpenBLAS is installed prior to building.
- Fix `CIBW_ENVIRONMENT_WINDOWS` quoting to a single-quoted string so `CMAKE_TOOLCHAIN_FILE=${{ env.VCPKG_INSTALLATION_ROOT }}\scripts\buildsystems\vcpkg.cmake` retains backslashes.

### Testing
- A prior automated `cibuildwheel` run on Windows failed with a CMake error stating it "Could not find toolchain file: scriptsbuildsystemsvcpkg.cmake".
- No automated CI workflow runs have been executed after this workflow change yet.
- No unit tests were run as part of this change.

author: Dmytro Mishkin
message:
Install OpenBLAS via vcpkg for Windows cibuildwheel builds

### Motivation
- Windows wheel builds failed because CMake could not find BLAS/LAPACK during `cibuildwheel` builds.
- Provide a reproducible way to install OpenBLAS on Windows CI using `vcpkg` so native dependencies are available.
- Pass the `vcpkg` toolchain configuration into the build so CMake can locate the installed libraries.

### Description
- Updated `.github/workflows/build_wheels.yml` to install OpenBLAS on Windows by setting `CIBW_BEFORE_ALL_WINDOWS: vcpkg install openblas --triplet x64-windows`.
- Set `CIBW_ENVIRONMENT_WINDOWS` to expose `VCPKG_ROOT` and `CMAKE_TOOLCHAIN_FILE` so the `vcpkg` toolchain is used by CMake during wheel builds.
- Kept `CIBW_ARCHS_WINDOWS` and `CIBW_BUILD` settings and the artifact upload/publish steps for Windows wheels.

### Testing
- A prior CI `cibuildwheel` run on Windows failed with a BLAS/LAPACK not found error as shown in the build logs.
- No automated tests were run after this workflow update because this is a CI workflow change and will be exercised on the next push/PR/tag trigger.
